name: Gefilte Fish--Pareve--
recipeInstructions:
1. Have a fishmonger skin, bone, and fillet the fish, reserving bones and heads for broth.
2. Rinse bones and heads. Place in a wide enamel-lined heavy Dutch oven.
3. Add 3 sliced onions, 4 sliced shallots, celery, carrots, parsnip, parsley, herbs, and lemon zest to the pot.
4. Add wine and water. Bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat to simmering. Cover and cool for 1 hour.
6. Using a fine sieve, strain into a large bowl. Pick out some carrot slices and set aside.
7. Press out juices from solids. Discard solids. Pour broth back into the pot and set aside.
8. Cut remaining onion into 1-inch pieces and shallots in half. Place in a food processor.
9. Rinse fish, and check for any remaining bones. Cut fish into 2-inch pieces and add to the processor with spices, parsley, and half the salt.
10. Process for 30 seconds. Add 1 whole egg and 1 white. Let stand while whipping the remaining whites until firm peaks form.
11. Set aside.
12. With the processor running, pour seltzer into the machine. Process for 15 seconds.
13. Scrape mixture into a large bowl. Sprinkle with 3 tablespoons matzo meal and blend. Fold in beaten egg whites. Cover and chill in the freezer for 15 minutes.
14. Bring broth to boil. Add remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt.
15. Using moistened hands and spoon, shape chilled mixture into 12 ovals, lowering them into the hot broth as they're formed.
16. Reduce heat to steady simmer. Cover and cook for 45 minutes, spooning ovals with broth after they've cooked for 10 minutes. Each piece will puff up and rise to the top.
17. Partially uncover and cool in the pot for 30 minutes.
18. Add reserved carrots to the broth. Cover and refrigerate overnight, or carefully transfer each piece to a container (s), pouring stock over all.
19. Tightly cover and refrigerate. The broth will thicken when chilled. If you prefer jelled broth, add 1 package of pareve unflavored gelatin to boiling stock and dissolve granules before refrigerating.
